strategies.name: High-Value Clusters First
strategies.description: Execute refactoring clusters in descending order of ranked score. Maximize ROI early.
strategies.rationale: Clusters are ranked by weighted score (benefitCount * 10 + effort + risk penalties). Executing highest-scoring clusters first delivers maximum value per unit effort. Enables early feedback + course correction.
parallelismWindows: L01: N agents scan in parallel (no inter-agent deps)
risks: High-effort clusters may block downstream work if they introduce bugs
Early execution of risky changes (even if high-value) can create technical debt
benefits: Delivers value early (high-score clusters = high benefit-to-effort ratio)
Early feedback loop; adjust subsequent clusters based on learnings
Natural stopping point if resources exhausted (completed highest-value work)

metrics.completionCriteria: All clusters in recommendedPlan executed + tests pass + perf targets met

strategies.name: Risky Changes First
strategies.description: Execute highest-risk clusters first (deep refactors, API changes). Discover blockers early.
strategies.rationale: High-risk changes (e.g., moving core modules, renaming APIs) introduce cascading errors if not caught early. Executing them first surfaces integration issues while low-value clusters can absorb fixes. De-risks later, safer changes.
strategies.executionOrder: clusters ordered by risk level (high → med → low), then by score within risk tier
parallelismWindows: L01: N agents scan in parallel
L03: spec generation with risk-priority ranking
risks: May discover showstopper issues mid-project; work wasted on low-value tasks if project halted
Longer time-to-value (high-risk clusters are often high-effort)
benefits: Discovers integration issues early (high-risk changes have many dependents)
Reduces rework cycles (fix blocking issues once, rest of changes build on stable base)
Builds confidence; later changes are lower-risk by definition

metrics.completionCriteria: All high/med-risk clusters executed + zero regression tests fail + integration tests pass

candidateSelection.reasoning: Maximizes near-term value delivery + provides natural stopping points. Risk mitigation in separate iteration (risk-first pass) if needed.
candidateSelection.alternativeReasoning: risk-first is safer for repos with deep coupling; choose risk-first if integration tests take >30min or if core modules are fragile

notes: Both strategies execute the same L00-L04 pipeline (survey → synthesis → spec generation).
Difference is in spec-kit generation (L03): which ranking order to use when generating TASKS.md?
risk-first extracts risk from SYNTHESIS clusters, re-ranks by risk level + score.
SELECTED.json will record which strategy was chosen; next iteration can compare results.
